Order Instituting Rulemaking for Oversight of Energy Efficiency Portfolios, Policies, Programs, and Evaluation. Application of Pacific Gas and Electric Company for Approval of 2024-2031 Energy Efficiency Business Plan and 2024-2027 Portfolio Plan.
* * *
**FILED**
And Other Matters. 08/13/25 04:59 PM R2504010
* * *
**BEFORE THE PUBLIC UTILITIES**
**COMMISSION OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA**
1
* * *
Rulemaking 25-04-010 (Filed April 24, 2025)
* * *
(NOT CONSOLIDATED) Application 22-02-005 (Filed February 15, 2022)
* * *
Application 22-03-003 Application 22-03-004 Application 22-03-005 Application 22-03-007 Application 22-03-008 Application 22-03-011 Application 22-03-012 (Consolidated)
* * *
**NORTHERN CALIFORNIA RURAL REGIONAL ENERGY NETWORK**
**NOTICE OF EX PARTE MEETING**
Pursuant to Rule 8.2 of the Rules of Practice and Procedure of the California Public Utilities Commission (Commission), Northern California Rural Regional Energy Network (NREN) provides this notice of an oral ex parte communication. On Monday, August 11, 2025 at 9:30 am, NREN met Kerry Fleisher, Chief Policy Advisor and Justin Galle, Energy Advisor to Commission President Reynolds. The meeting was initiated at the request of NREN, was held via WebEx, and lasted approximately 30 minutes. The meeting was attended by Patricia Terry, Senior Portfolio Manager, Redwood Coast Energy Authority (RCEA)/Northern Rural Energy Network; Stephen Kullmann, Director, Demand Side Management, Order Instituting Rulemaking for Oversight of Energy Efficiency Portfolios, Policies, Programs, and Evaluation. Application of Pacific Gas and Electric Company for Approval of 2024-2031 Energy Efficiency Business Plan and 2024-2027 Portfolio Plan.
* * *
And Other Matters.
* * *
2 Customer Programs, RCEA; and Susie Berlin, Law Offices of Susie Berlin, attorney for NREN. Following introductions, Ms. Berlin provided an overview of the purpose of the meeting, noting that NREN supports the Commission’s adoption of Draft Resolution E-5400, Rev. 1, which approves the NREN and Central California Rural Regional Energy Network (CCR REN) Advice Letter filings with the revised 2024-27 energy efficiency portfolio business plans for NREN and CCR REN. Ms. Berlin noted that the Draft Resolution was issued in May and placed on CPUC meeting agenda for June 12; it has since been held several times for further review. NREN supports approval of the Advice Letter filings as reflected in Revision 1. Ms. Berlin noted that the revised draft addresses some of the concerns that both NREN and CCR REN had raised in comments on the initial Draft Resolution, and in particular language that could imply that there is duplication between NREN and ESA residential programs. NREN agrees with CCR REN further proposed changes to the Draft Resolution as they help to clarify any potential ambiguity. She observed that the ongoing delay in approving the Advice Letters was compromising NREN’s ability to provide these important residential programs to the customers that need them the most. Mr. Kullman noted that RCEA is the lead administrator for NREN, and that NREN covers rural areas in the northern part of the state and Sierra Nevada region; customers are widely dispersed, hard-to-reach, and include many tribal communities. It was noted that NREN’s outreach efforts are crucial in this area because traditional energy efficiency program providers have customarily not served these areas, and residential customers in particular, because doing so is not cost-effective. Overall, the programs and measures NREN provides, and particularly those for residential customers, are intended to support the hardest-to-reach customers in the state. Mr. Kullman further noted concern that the delay presents another delay in getting equity programs to the residential customers that need these services the most. Mr. Kullman and Ms. Berlin both noted
* * *
3 that the lack of final approval of the underlying NREN Advice Letter is creating uncertainty around the launch and implementation of the residential programs; even preparation of the 2028-2031 Business Plan becomes uncertain. Ms. Berlin underscored the distinction between overlap in program outreach and duplication of program offerings; she noted that the Draft Resolution accurately identifies factors that already provide coordination and oversight to prevent duplication. Ms. Terry provided additional context from an implementation perspective regarding the distinction between overlap in outreach and duplication. Ms. Terry noted that NREN overlaps and interacts with the same customers as ESA, however, that overlap does not result in those customers receiving duplicative services. While customers may be eligible for both an NREN and ESA program, they would not receive both programs, and NREN does refer ESA-eligible customers to the ESA-provider with the customers’ consent. Ms. Terry also noted that NREN is in a position to continue outreach to customers, and even compliment ESA and fill gaps in ESA administrators’ ability to reach customers. This is because there are some limitations on the ESA programs that are not the same for NREN programs, for example where a customer is switching from propane or another fuel source, rather than from natural gas. Ms. Terry underscored that the NREN programs are complimentary to ESA and help to reach the truly hardest-to-reach customers. Ms. Berlin noted that the residential programs at issue had previously been approved by the Commission as part of initial Rural REN business plan filings prior to the two rural RENs being bifurcated in D.12-09-031; the same programs were included in the separate Advice Letter filings of NREN and CCR REN, as directed by the Commission in that decision. Ms. Berlin further noted that delays in approving the Draft Resolution create ambiguity regarding the residential programs at issue. Mr. Kullman noted that while NREN is working with
* * *
4 PG&E; on the required Joint Cooperation Memorandum, PG&E; is reticent to finalize the agreement without final approval of the NREN Business Plan and Budget, which would occur with approval of Draft Resolution E-5400. Ms. Berlin concluded by extending an offer to address any additional questions the Commissioner’s Office may have (following appropriate ex parte rules) and urged adoption of the Draft Resolution E-5400 Rev. 1 as soon as possible.
* * *
Dated August 13, 2025 Respectfully submitted,
* * *
C. Susie Berlin L AW OFFICES OF SUSIE BERLIN
* * *
